title: Doximity Stock Just Got Crushed. Is This a Rare Chance to Buy a High-Quality Growth Company on Sale?
author: Josh Kohn-Lindquist
description: Doximity stock has plummeted 56% in 2026 amid concerns that AI could disrupt its medical platform services. However, the author argues these fears are overblown, citing the company's dominant 85%+ physician network, strong customer base (top 20 pharma companies and hospital systems), and regulatory barriers that protect it from easy disruption. Trading at 15x free cash flow, the author views it as a buying opportunity as pharma advertising spending should eventually rebound.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Despite the 56% stock decline, the author presents a bullish case arguing AI fears are overdone. The company's strong ecosystem (85%+ physician adoption, top pharma and hospital customers), regulatory moats, and attractive valuation (15x FCF) make it a compelling buy opportunity as the market has overreacted to AI competition concerns.

title: Dow Jones Rally Looks Fragile With Inflation Data Running Hot
author: Itai Smidt
description: The Dow Jones climbed back above 50,000 driven by Cisco's strong earnings beat and guidance raise, while Nvidia gained on news of Chinese H200 chip approvals. However, the rally appears fragile as inflation data runs hot with import prices spiking 1.9% and energy costs surging, offsetting positive consumer resilience metrics. The Trump-Xi summit in Beijing provided mild sentiment support but delivered limited concrete deliverables.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Delivered strong fiscal Q3 earnings beat with $1.16-$1.18 adjusted EPS vs $1.07 consensus and $16.7-$16.9B revenue vs $15.82B expected. Announced strategic workforce reduction and repositioning as AI infrastructure play, driving 13-15% stock surge.

Gained 2-2.5% on regulatory approval for Chinese firms to purchase H200 accelerators, reducing China overhang that has persisted for two years. CEO Jensen Huang's presence at Beijing summit reinforces thaw in U.S.-China relations.
Co-led chip rally on Wednesday with strength extending into memory sector alongside logic chips, benefiting from broader semiconductor momentum.
Down 19-23% after Q4 adjusted EPS of $0.26 missed $0.28 consensus and both Q1 and full-year revenue guidance came in below expectations, triggering worst performer status.
Fell 8-9% on adjusted net income of $20.3M vs $23.9M expected and adjusted revenue of $92.8M vs $94.9M estimate, missing on both earnings and revenue lines.
Surged 13-14% on Q1 revenue of $446M and adjusted EBITDA of $72.1M, both comfortably exceeding Street estimates of $432M and $65.1M respectively.
Jumped 10-12% after adjusted EPS of $0.26 crushed $0.18 estimate and revenue of $380.4M exceeded $374.7M expected, delivering decisive beats on both metrics.

title: Why Doximity Stock Plunged Today
author: Josh Kohn-Lindquist
description: Doximity stock fell 24% after reporting mixed Q4 2026 earnings with only 5% sales growth and disappointing 2027 guidance of 3-5% revenue growth. The company is increasing AI spending amid a soft healthcare advertising market, pressuring profitability. Despite industry headwinds, Doximity maintains its market leadership with 85% of U.S. physicians on its platform and trades at a cheap 12x forward earnings valuation.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Stock plunged 24% due to underwhelming Q4 earnings that barely met sales expectations while missing on earnings, combined with weak 2027 revenue guidance of only 3-5% growth and declining adjusted EBITDA margins. However, the author notes the company is not 'doomed' operationally and maintains strong market position with cheap valuation at 12x forward earnings.

title: Stock Market Today: S&P 500, Dow, Nasdaq Futures Gain As Trump Clears NVDA's H200 Sales To China— Cisco Systems, Doximity In Focus (UPDATED)
author: Rishabh Mishra
description: U.S. stock futures rose on Thursday as President Trump addresses trade issues with China. Cisco Systems surged 18.8% on better-than-expected Q3 results and raised FY26 guidance. Doximity plunged 21.46% on mixed Q4 results and weak guidance. Grocery Outlet gained 16.41% after beating EPS estimates by 150%. The Fed is expected to hold rates steady in June with 98.6% probability. Nvidia's H200 chip sales to Chinese firms were cleared by the U.S., though Beijing halted deliveries.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Soared 18.8% in premarket after reporting better-than-expected Q3 financial results and raising FY26 guidance. Strong price trend across all timeframes.
Plunged 21.46% after reporting mixed Q4 results and issuing weak Q1 sales guidance. Weak long and medium-term price trends.
Gained 16.41% after reporting Q1 results with EPS beating analyst estimates by 150%. Strong short-term price trend.
Shares zoomed 16.96% after posting Q3 revenue of $8.7 million, more than double year-over-year.
Up 2.01% with strong price trends across all timeframes and solid quality score. Expected to report earnings of $2.66 per share.
U.S. cleared H200 AI chip sales to Chinese firms, but Beijing halted deliveries, creating mixed signals. Up 2.25% in premarket.

title: Stock Market Today: Nasdaq 100 Rises Despite Hot PPI, Nvidia Hits Record High
author: Piero Cingari
description: Tech stocks surged on Wednesday despite a hotter-than-expected April PPI reading (6% YoY vs 4.8% expected), which rekindled inflation concerns. The Nasdaq 100 gained 0.7% led by semiconductor strength, while rate-sensitive sectors like utilities and financials declined. Nvidia hit record highs above $226 ahead of earnings, Ford surged 11.9% on strong Q1 results, and software stocks like Dynatrace and Doximity fell sharply on disappointing earnings.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Stock rallied for sixth consecutive session to above $226, market cap exceeded $5.5 trillion, and CEO Jensen Huang joining Trump's China delegation signals strategic importance and investor confidence ahead of earnings
